_readme.what_this_is: Archetype MANIFEST for a personal / portfolio / content site. Resolve with `node library/resolve.mjs archetype.portfolio.json`.
_readme.reuse: Still 100% composed from the shared library — ZERO portfolio-specific checkpoints. The design.* checkpoints are the same ones the ecommerce archetype uses; the web.* checkpoints are shared with any content site; and it draws the universal security.*/data.*/reliability.* checkpoints every deployed site needs (HTTPS + headers, secrets, PII on the contact form, injection, error handling) — auth/upload/query checkpoints are omitted since a portfolio has no accounts or backend to speak of. The strongest demonstration of the library: a whole archetype with nothing new to author.
_readme.criticals: A portfolio has no revenue backbone, but it still carries BREACH-class criticals the moment it has any dynamic surface: a secret shipped to the client (a leaked CMS/API token) and injection through a contact form are ship-blocking on a portfolio exactly as on a store. Those two are marked severity:critical here. On a pure-static site that has neither, the adversarial N/A verdict grades them 'not applicable' — honestly absent, NEVER a silent pass. Not reviewing and not reporting are the same failure; this archetype refuses to look clean by having nothing to fail.
_readme.design_is_the_product_but_hard_to_grade: The design + performance bar is the portfolio's actual product, kept at severity:high. BUT design is graded best-effort from source and is the least reliable dimension that way — a trustworthy design/a11y verdict needs the instrumented browser probe (`forespec design <url>`). The report must say so rather than imply a clean static read is the final word.
_readme.how_scoring_works: Levels 3/6/9. 3 = crude/risky. 6 = solid, shippable. 9 = great. TRUST THE GAP, NOT THE NUMBER.

applies_when: A personal, portfolio, or content site: mostly static content, a high design bar, an optional contact form, little or no backend state.

goal_definition.shippable: no severity:critical below level 6 (criticals grade N/A on a pure-static site with no dynamic surface), and all severity:high at level >= 6
goal_definition.great: all severity:critical at 9, all severity:high at 9, all others >= 6
goal_definition.honest_ceiling: design checkpoints tagged taste_limited verify to ~level 7 mechanically; reaching 9 requires human design review the rubric cannot replace, and a trustworthy design read requires the browser probe, not source grading.
